# OpenProgression

> An open standard for fitness progression assessment. 7 levels, 8 categories, 25 benchmarks derived from 1.3 million data points. Free and MIT licensed.

OpenProgression provides research-backed benchmarks for assessing fitness across all major movement domains. Each benchmark has male and female standards at 7 progression levels mapped to population percentiles.

## Links

- [Home](https://openprogression.org)
- [The 7 Levels](https://openprogression.org/levels)
- [8 Categories](https://openprogression.org/categories)
- [Benchmarks](https://openprogression.org/benchmarks)
- [Calculator](https://openprogression.org/calculator)
- [Programming](https://openprogression.org/programming)
- [Data & Downloads](https://openprogression.org/data)
- [Methodology](https://openprogression.org/methodology)
- [License](https://openprogression.org/license)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/davidborgenvik/openprogression)

## API / Data Access

All data is available as JSON at:
- Bundled: https://openprogression.org/data/openprogression.json
- Levels: https://openprogression.org/data/levels.json
- Categories: https://openprogression.org/data/categories.json
- Benchmarks: https://openprogression.org/data/benchmarks/{category}.json

## Full Documentation

- [llms-full.txt](https://openprogression.org/llms-full.txt)
